---
title: Hydrophone Life Cycle
---

This section outlines the end-to-end journey of a hydrophone at ONC, from the initial purchase and contract to long-term data analysis.
Thie life cycle is based on Aghaei et al., 2024: **Best Practices for Managing Passive Acoustics Data at Ocean Networks Canada**.

Hydrophones have been in use at ONC since 2006, totalling more than 700 TB of archived data.

The life cycle workflow ensures that data is not just collected, but are **FAIR** (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable).
ONC commits to share and preserve data long-term. 


[INSERT DIAGRAM OF LIFE CYCLE/DATA FLOW]


# 1. Planning

*Teams involved: Science, DAQ, DSO, Engineers*

Before a hydrophone is deployed in the water, the following legal and technical steps must be taken: 

* **Data Agreements**: ONC establishes legal agreements to define data ownership, usage restrictions, and attribution.
* **Stewardship**: Data Stewards (DSO) apply standardized metadata and attributions to ensure the sensor is *discoverable*.
* **Product Definition**: We identify what data products (e.g., spectrograms, MAT files) the community needs.


* The need for new or updated data products is identified here.


data ownership?



# Data Collection

*Main teams involved: DAQ, DSO, Engineers*

* single hydrophone or hydrophone array tests calibrations (HydroCal)
* post-recovery calibrations is also performed and compared to pre-deplopyment values. if inconsistencies, device may be returned to manufacturer.
* hydrophones are categorized as Tier 1 instruments: instruments that are of concern to the Royal Canadian Navy (RCN) and US Navy (USN). Therefore, data stream may be subject to data diversion before sent to ONC. 
* main manufacturers: GeoSpectrum Technologies and Ocean Sonics, deployed as single element or four element hydrophone arrays.
* icHF (HF=High Frequency) deployed with sample rates of 60k, 128k or 256k S/s.
* icAF deployed at 32k S/s.
* communication and power supply tests are performed
* once a hydrophone is deployed, raw acoustic data is transferred directly (or after diversion) to ONC archive file servers (the servers are a UVic).
* Ancillary (also auxiliiary) data are transmitted, without diversion. this data is *instrument health*, including tempreature, internal humidity, battery, internal clock drift.
* data processing is performed to generate Spectral Data (HSD) in MAT format, and Spectral Density Plots (HSDP) from .wav or .flac files which are archived.
* data becomes readily available to end-users thorugh Oceans 3.0.

signal chain, driver

# Data Quality Assurance

*Main teams involved: DAQ, Science, Systems*

* At this time, there is no QAQC procedures for hydrophone data, because it is considered **complex data** (per QuartoD manual).
* manual (visual) Quality Assurance is done periodically (ideally daily) to identify clipping, masking, interference, energy spikes, but the data is not modified.
* annotations in Oceans 3.0 can be made for anything unsual seen in the data
* data gaps occur often, due to either instrument failure, connectivity issues, communication down, network maintenance, data diversion. it is up to the hydrophone team to identify the root cause and annnotate data gaps.

# Commissioning

*Teams involved: DAQ, DSO*

# Metadata

*Main teams involved: DSO, DAQ*

* each hydrophone type (AF, HF, etc?) has several underlying metadata which contain high-level information about network details, baud rate, data products, calirbration processes, etc
* per FAIR principles, and IOOS categories, SeaDataNet Device Categories, and SeaVox Device Catalogue.
* metadata adhere to the ISO. 19115 standards.


# 5. PRESERVE

*Main teams involved: Software, DSO, Systems*

* terabytes of raw acoustic data have been stored locally at University of Victoria data center.
* also data products (SPD, etc), daily log files, MAT files.
* offsite complete backup also (where? manitoba?)


# Access the data
* Data search within Oceans3.0 can be filtered by device location, device category (Hydrophone), time windows, 
* choice between clean and raw (??? are we talking about the aux data?)
* 5-min segments of spectrogram avaialble in the Hydrophoen Data Viewer
* Oceans3.0 API is an easier way to access the data
* each dataset has a DOI


# Analyse

*Teams involved: Science, DAQ*

# WORKFLOWS
* each events in a hydrophone life cycle is recorded as a *device action*, such as deplyoment, recovery, calibrations

# Recovery

*Teams involved: DSO, MarineOps, Systems*


# 9. DECOMMISSIONING (GOOD BYE)

*Team involved: DSO*
